_comment: E10-07 revision paraphrase matrix (R8 item 1). Every phrasing is a change request, correction, addition, or concern at the spec review gate — many without any revision prefix, several phrased as questions or wrapped in soft approval language (the E10-01 'fine, but…' ambiguity traps). Each must leave the workflow at spec-draft and re-dispatch the spec author; none may ever reach spec-approved (the safety half of default-to-revision). Synthetic fixtures; no exact hook phrases. The `hgp-*` cases are the SHARED corpus driven verbatim by test/e2e/human-gate-protocol.e2e.test.mjs (issue #3): the E2E suite proves the deterministic Stage-1 listener DEFERS these to the LLM stage without moving the gate; here the simulated Stage-2 interpreter classifies them as revise-artifact — so both layers see identical text and neither ever advances a human gate on them. The trailing-prose approval 'approve spec — but rename the module later' is deliberately E2E-ONLY: Stage 1 emits a near-miss hint and defers, but the Stage-2 interpreter reads the trailing 'later' as a non-blocking nit and WOULD approve — the exact divergence that is why an inexact approval must reach the LLM/human rather than auto-advance.
